lines.text: - Naitamba Island -
Two hours by boat from Suva. The main island of the group.
This is where the water project stopped ten years ago.
- Naitamba Island -
Two hours by boat from Suva. The main island of the group.
You came before government approval. You are underprepared.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Welcome.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: They said you came from KODA.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Welcome.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: They said you came from KODA.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Powder. Some people use this nowadays.
It was not the traditional form, but Ratu accepts it.
The powder is mixed with water in the tanoa. The sevusevu begins.
The bilo goes around. One clap before receiving, drink, then three claps.
A quiet time passes.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: There are things I remember. The promise ten years ago. Nothing remained.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Which number are you?

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Welcome.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: They said you came from KODA.

choices.text: "I brought a gift." (You present the mango)

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Mango.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Usually, yaqona is what you bring here.
An awkward silence passes.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: But you did not come empty-handed from far away. I appreciate the gesture.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Sit. I should at least hear you out.
There was no yaqona, but Ratu opens a space for conversation.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: There are things I remember. The promise ten years ago. Nothing remained.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Which number are you?

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Welcome.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: They said you came from KODA.

choices.text: "Hello. It's an honor to meet you."

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...You came knowing the custom.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Come in. Let us sit together.
Everyone sits in a circle inside the bure. A tanoa, a wooden bowl, rests in the center.
You hand over the yaqona root. A young man kneels and presents it to Ratu.
Ratu nods. The young man begins to pound the yaqona.
The powdered yaqona is strained through cloth and mixed with water in the tanoa. A grey-brown liquid fills it.
A bilo, a half-coconut cup, is offered. You clap once before receiving it.
You drink it in one go. Bitter, earthy. Your tongue tingles slightly.
You clap three times. The bilo moves to the next person.
Quiet time passes. No one is in a hurry.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: There are things I remember. The promise ten years ago. Nothing remained.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Which number are you?

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...I see.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: We have more to say, but ask the village people too.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Lani. That young person will have something to tell you.
Ratu points toward Lani.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...I see.
A short silence passes.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Sit. You came a long way, so I should hear you out.
There was no yaqona, but a place for conversation opens.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: There are things I remember. The promise ten years ago. Nothing remained.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Which number are you?

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: ...Greet Ratu first. Here, that's the order.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: ...You're the person from Suva, right?

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: Let me ask one thing. This project - did anyone ask us? Whether this is what we want?

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: The news always says 'community demand confirmed.'

choices.text: "I only saw it in the file. I came to hear it directly."
"It is report-based, but I want to hear it again."

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: ...You're the first person to say it like that.
Lani glances at Ratu.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: What we want is not simply a new tank.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Ten years ago they said they would build a tank too. When it broke, no one could fix it. The pipes are still old, and every dry season the same problem returns.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: What we want is this: someone who remains responsible afterward.
It is hard to answer.

choices.text: "This time, we'll build a structure the village can manage directly."

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: So you knew it was report-based.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: In the dry season, half the village loses water pressure because the pipes are old.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: Yes, we need a tank. But that is not the whole problem.
Ratu nods.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: A tank was installed ten years ago. The pipes burst after two years. We requested repairs, but no one came from Suva.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: You build and leave. We are the ones who remain.

choices.text: "We'll build a structure the village can manage directly."

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: A survey.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: You mean the boxes someone from Suva came and checked?

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Let's stop here today.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Ourselves.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: No one said that ten years ago.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Good. I will watch once more.
For the first time, it feels like a door has opened.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Ourselves.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: No one said that ten years ago.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Good. I will watch once more.
For the first time, it feels like a door has opened.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.text: The Land Office gave me the consent form. If you sign here, the project can officially proceed.
Ratu reads the document slowly.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: There is a village management committee clause. This is new.
Ratu picks up the pen and signs.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: This signature is a promise to the village people. Not to you.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Do not forget that.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Your best.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Your predecessor said the same thing.
Ratu stands up.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Bring yaqona next time too.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: Earlier, you said the village would manage it directly.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: There is one technician in our village. Uncle Josua. He knows pipes.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: Ten years ago, he asked headquarters for repair approval and was told it was 'not allowed by procedure.' That's why the tank rusted.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: If village people can repair it directly... maybe it really will be different.

choices.text: "I'll put Uncle Josua on the management committee. Could you join too, Lani?"

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: ...Me too?

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: If that's real... yes. I'll try.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: ...Okay.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: I hope it works out. This time.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: I will wait.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Ah, you're here. I was about to contact you.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: I came ahead as we discussed and investigated. I've spent a few days listening to villagers.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Bottom line: during the project ten years ago, the contractor chose the location without consulting the village.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: The tank was built in the middle of someone's field, and one family couldn't farm there anymore.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: People were angry not just because the tank broke, but because no one asked their opinion.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: When you greet Ratu Josefa, it is important to show that the village has decision-making power.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: And - did you bring yaqona? You need it for the sevusevu ceremony.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: If you didn't, it is inconvenient, but you should return to Suva and buy some. Going without yaqona changes the first impression.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Make sure you speak to Lani too. Lani leads the village youth group.

choices.text: "This is useful. Could you share your field findings later?"

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Then greet Ratu Josefa first, and make sure you speak with Lani. I'll keep investigating here.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Listen carefully to people here. These stories aren't in the documents.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: The fastest way is to hear directly what went wrong ten years ago.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Did you see Lani earlier? Lani leads the village youth group.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Lani has influence among the residents. Winning Lani over is key.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: That went well. Ratu Josefa does not open the door to many people.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: I'll share my findings: there is a young technician in the village named Josua.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: If he is included in the management committee, the maintenance problem can be solved.

choices.text: "Thanks to you, I can see the picture. Thank you, truly."

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Go to the Land Office and meet Sela. I'll keep investigating here.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: This may take time.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Don't rush building trust with residents.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.text: Ratu Josefa, the Land Office says a village consent form is required.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.text: With your signature, the project approval process can move forward.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Consent form.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: I signed something similar ten years ago.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: After that signature, what changed? Nothing.

choices.text: "This time, the village manages it directly. Village youth will join the committee."
"I respect your judgment. If you need time, I'll give it."

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Good.
Ratu Josefa reads the document slowly.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: There is a village management committee clause. This is new.

lines.speaker: Lani
lines.text: Ratu, they said I'm joining the committee too.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...Is that so?
Ratu looks at Lani and smiles briefly.
Ratu picks up the pen and signs.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: This signature is a promise to the village people. Not to you.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Do not forget that.
A brief silence passes.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...I will not tell you to wait.
Ratu begins to read the document.
Ratu picks up the pen and signs.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: This signature is a promise to the village people. Not to you.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: Do not forget that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.text: Ratu Josefa, the Land Office requested the consent form.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: ...I still do not know what you can promise.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: I cannot sign yet.
You did not receive the consent form.

lines.speaker: Ratu Josefa
lines.text: I signed. Keep your promise.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: You got the signature?

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: That's huge. Ratu Josefa signing is not easy.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Now return to the government building in Suva and submit this consent form to Sela on the fifth floor.

lines.speaker: Mere
lines.text: Then the project officially begins.
